2604.07558.title: Generative Experiences for Digital Mental Health Interventions: Evidence from a Randomized Study
2604.07558.paper_summary: Digital mental health (DMH) tools have extensively explored personalization of interventions to users' needs and contexts. However, this personalization often targets what support is provided, not how it is experienced. Even well-matched content can fail when the interaction format misaligns with how someone can engage. We introduce generative experience as a paradigm for DMH support, where the intervention experience is composed at runtime. We instantiate this in GUIDE, a system that generates personalized intervention content and multimodal interaction structure through rubric-guided generation of modular components. In a preregistered study with N = 237 participants, GUIDE significantly reduced stress (p = .02) and improved the user experience (p = .04) compared to an LLM-based cognitive restructuring control. GUIDE also supported diverse forms of reflection and action through varied interaction flows, while revealing tensions around personalization across the interaction sequence. This work lays the foundation for interventions that dynamically shape how support is experienced and enacted in digital settings.
2604.07558.paper_summary_zh: 數位心理健康 (DMH) 工具已廣泛探索根據用戶的需求和情境進行干預的個性化。<lb/>然而，這種個性化通常針對提供哪些支持，而不是如何體驗這些支持。<lb/>即使是匹配良好的內容，當互動格式與某人能夠參與的方式不一致時，也可能失敗。<lb/>我們引入生成體驗作為 DMH 支持的一種範式，在這種範式中，干預體驗是在運行時組成的。<lb/>我們在 GUIDE 中實現了這一點，該系統通過基於評分標準的模塊化組件生成個性化的干預內容和多模態互動結構。<lb/>在一項預註冊的研究中，N = 237 名參與者，GUIDE 顯著降低了壓力 (p = .02) 並改善了用戶體驗 (p = .04)，與基於 LLM 的認知重組對照組相比。<lb/>GUIDE 還通過多樣的互動流程支持各種反思和行動形式，同時揭示了在互動序列中圍繞個性化的緊張關係。<lb/>這項工作為在數位環境中動態塑造支持的體驗和執行方式的干預奠定了基礎。
2604.07558.author: Ananya Bhattacharjee et.al.
2604.07558.authors: Ananya Bhattacharjee, Michael Liut, Matthew Jörke, Diyi Yang, Emma Brunskill
